name: Garlic-Rosemary Oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and cut the garlic into very thin slices.
2. Pour the oil into a small heavy skillet or saucepan.
3. Add the sliced garlic and crush the rosemary between your fingers as you add it.
4. Warm over low heat until the aroma rises and the garlic just starts to take on color (it should not brown), about 5-7 minutes.
5. Remove from the heat, then add the salt to taste.
6. Strain the oil into a small pitcher.
NOTE: The oil can be made up to 2 weeks ahead, cooled to room temperature, and stored in the refrigerator in a tightly sealed container. To enjoy the full flavor and fragrance of the oil, make sure to bring it to room temperature before using. Drizzle over potato waffles.
